axis_semantics.hrf_basis: haemodynamic response basis for the GLM convolution (canonical SPM, canonical + temporal/dispersion derivatives, Glover, or FIR).
axis_semantics.confounds: nuisance-regressor model: 6 motion params, 24 motion (Friston), +physio (aCompCor/global), +pupil, or both.
axis_semantics.high_pass: cosine-drift high-pass cutoff in seconds (128 or 256).

notes.coverage: normalized Shannon entropy over the FULL axis space (NOT the prior-restricted options), clamped to 1.0 per axis, so a near-delta prior LOWERS coverage rather than hiding a collapsed multiverse.
notes.mean_efficiency: eligibility FLOOR only (a design whose contrast is unestimable is ineligible); NEVER maximized. A high mean efficiency is a detection-power proxy for the PRE-CHOSEN contrast, so maximizing it would be a back door to result-favourable selection.
notes.efficiency_stability: the MAXIMIZED robustness signal = across-variant concordance 1/(1+cv) of the per-variant efficiency. It reads only event TIMING + synthetic confound STRUCTURE (regressor COUNT), never confounds.tsv VALUES or any z-map/beta.
notes.count_efficiency_proxy: events-free, value-free structural DOF proxy 1/(1 + n_confound_regressors + hrf_extra_regressors); used offline when raw events.tsv is unavailable. For the real events-based nilearn design-matrix efficiency, use the server-backed optimizer (efficiency_fn built from event timing).
notes.duplicate_variants: a committed multiverse is a SET of DISTINCT pipelines; duplicate variant ids are de-duplicated so they cannot inflate the variant count, coverage, or stability.
notes.objective_safety: the fitness reads only the DESIGN (decision points, the contrast vector, event timing). Two different confounds.tsv files give byte-identical scores; that byte-identity is the load-bearing CI invariant.
